id: b666555f-ebef-53bb-ab6f-20bfd80e2ccd
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to FBI Warns TeamPCP Hackers Compromise Developer Tools in Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks (Web Application).

    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: SUPPLYCHAIN
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/FBI Warns TeamPCP Hackers Compromise Developer Tools in Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — FBI Warns TeamPCP Hackers Compromise Developer Tools in Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T13:56:27Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "FBI Warns TeamPCP Hackers Compromise Developer Tools in Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX FBI Warns TeamPCP Hackers Compromise Developer Tools in Large-Scale Supply Chain Attacks Exploitation Attempt - SUPPLYCHAIN"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6446632; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — JADEPUFFER First End-to-End AI-Driven Ransomware Operation
id: f6ac71e7-f8fa-50bf-8e3a-21a8d7abaea0
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to JADEPUFFER: First End-to-End AI-Driven Ransomware Operation (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: RANSOMWARE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/JADEPUFFER: First End-to-End AI-Driven Ransomware Operation
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_encrypt:
        TargetFilename|endswith:
            - '.locked'
            - '.encrypted'
            - '.crypted'
            - '.enc'
        EventID: 11
    selection_ransomnote:
        TargetFilename|contains:
            - 'README'
            - 'HOW_TO_DECRYPT'
            - 'RECOVER_FILES'
    selection_vssdelete:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'vssadmin delete shadows'
            - 'wmic shadowcopy delete'
            - 'bcdedit /set'
    condition: selection_encrypt or selection_ransomnote or selection_vssdelete
falsepositives:
    - Encryption software (VeraCrypt, BitLocker) in use
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — JADEPUFFER: First End-to-End AI-Driven Ransomware Operation — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T13:56:27Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "JADEPUFFER: First End-to-End AI-Driven Ransomware Operation", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX JADEPUFFER: First End-to-End AI-Driven Ransomware Operation Exploitation Attempt - RANSOMWARE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"vssadmin"; nocase; content:"delete shadows"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:4910690; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw Under Active Attack, 950 Systems Exposed
items.description: Oracle E-Business Suite flaw CVE-2026-46817 is under active attack, with about 950 vulnerable internet-facing instances still exposed. This week, Defused Cyber researchers warned that a critical vulnerability in Oracle E-Business Suite, tracked as CVE-2026-46817, is being actively exploited. The flaw affects Oracle Payments versions 12.2.3 through 12.2.15 and allows unauthenticated attackers to take over vulnerable […]

items.title: CVE-2026-58028: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Wikimedia F
items.description:
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Wikimedia Foundation MediaWiki, Wikimedia Foundation CentralAuth.

 This vulnerability is associated with program files includes/Api/ApiFormatBase.Php, includes/Api/ApiHelp.Php, includes/ResourceLoader/Module.Php, includes/Hooks/Handlers/PageDisplayHookHandler.Php, includes/LogFormatter/PermissionChangeLogFormatter.Php.



This issue affects MediaWiki: from * before 1.46.0, 1.45.4, 1.44.6, 1.43.9; CentralAuth: from * before 1.46.0, 1.45.4, 1.44.6, 1.43.9.

title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-58030 Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation XSS o
id: 0b4fc2e7-0d96-55be-9e41-76d89c8ca79f
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-58030 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: XSS
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-58030
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: webserver
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-58030 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T13:56:27Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-58030", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-58030 Exploitation Attempt - XSS"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:8039994; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — EPSS score 0.390 (elevated exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title:
CVE-2026-58027: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor vulnerability in Wikimedia Foundation AbuseFilter.

 This vul
items.description:
Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor vulnerability in Wikimedia Foundation AbuseFilter.

 This vulnerability is associated with program files includes/Api/QueryAbuseFilters.Php.



This issue affects AbuseFilter: from * before 1.46.0, 1.45.4, 1.44.6, 1.43.9.
items.apex_ai_summary: [MEDIUM] CVE advisory. Risk score 5.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

apex.ai_summary: [P3] Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 5.0/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: STANDARD PRIORITY - Schedule patch within 7 days. Deploy detection rules. Monitor SIEM for related indicators.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 7.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.title: golang.org/x/image/tiff has excessive resource consumption in PackBits decompression
items.description: The TIFF decoder does not place a limit on the size of PackBits-compressed data. A maliciously-crafted image can exploit this to cause a small image (both in terms of pixel width/height and encoded size) to make the decoder decode large amounts of compressed data.
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
